I appreciate how you started off the video with this issue. It has been driving me insane.

Hmmm, I wonder if I just never noticed because I swap modes fairly frequently.

The transparency mode will always be my killer feature for these things. Comes in super clutch for staying sane at busy venues while being able to chat when needed.

the hollow feeling is gone for me with the latest update finally.
Happened when a fan was nearby
i love the case because it fits perfectly in those little pockets in jeans originaly meant for pocketwatches
My gen 2 pods are slowly deteriorating and losing quality this was well needed

Great review. That’s funny how we all come away with different ideas about what major reviewers said. I remember the overwhelming opinion being the noise canceling was better, but the sound quality was WORSE. They almost universally like the sound profile of the 2’s over the 3’s.
The speaker on the case has saved me so much time!
Great review video. I would love reviews of B and O speakers and headphones if possible.

puh-tee-nah. I've had mine since Christmas and would never have imagined my pockets would've scuffed the case this much. Patina. So you know I’ve watched the entire video 😃 Very interesting review, appreciate it. I don’t think I have noticed this issue, but maybe I haven’t been paying enough attention. very thorough review  Thank you.  I am an apple user and have thought about AirPods and the AirPod 3 pro is what I have been looking at!

This ANC degradation issue bugs me for the last 3 months or so. I observed its pattern and usually the howling effect in mine occurs when the airpods battery drops below 80%, I noticed that when my left airpod is for is at lower battery percentage such as 60+ percent and I take out the right airpod with 100 percent battery, the howling only happens to the earbud that has lower battery which is at that time my left pair.

I almost never use ANC on any of my earbuds or headphones so I wouldn’t know if my APP 3 is effected by the bug mentioned here, other than that they have been great earbuds for me and I’m glad I have them. No regrets here

For me, the anc issue can only be fixed when I switch to the off mode, then back to anc, and even then, it only works for less than an hour before it comes back. Also, it only happens in one AirPod while the other seems to work perfectly. I’m going to try and get them into the Apple Store to see if I can’t get them repaired or replaced.

I have never heard this ANC noise to be honest. I have loved them so much. Also, I only wear them when I’m listening to something though so even if there were a faint noise, I don’t feel like I would hear that.

What drives me crazy is the high pitched sound that comes from them when I take the right one off. It happens with both but if you put them in your hand and shuffle with them a little you’ll be able to see what I mean. It happens to me if I take them out too fast. THAT is driving me insane because it actually hurts my hearing. It didn’t happen to any of the 2s I bought previously. 😢 I thought I got a faulty pair too so I returned them and the same thing happened.

As a person who uses his AirPod pros a lot I do not notice that big of a difference between the twos and the threes when it comes to noise cancellation maybe it’s 15% better…. I think I actually preferred the sound quality of the two slightly better.  But the comfort level for me on the threes is quite a bit better just my two cents.
ranked.transcript: When the initial AirPods Pro 3 reviews came out, every major reviewer basically said the same thing. The sound quality was excellent and the noise cancellation was quite improved from the AirPods Pro 2. But all of them ended up missing the biggest downside that I've run into with the AirPods Pro 3, which didn't show up until several weeks after launch, which is exactly why I review things over the long term, because some issues just don't show up in 2 weeks. So, what is this big issue with the AirPods Pro 3? Well, a few weeks into using them constantly, I started to notice weird background sounds while using ANC. It sounds kind of hollow, almost like this oceanlike noise, as if there was like just some sound leakage problem with the AirPods Pro 3. And I'd notice this with the sound of my own voice when I'm writing at my Mac and reading out scripts to myself or the noise of just typing on a keyboard or weirdly a sound like rustling a plastic bag. That's something that definitely triggers it as well. Now, all of these were sounds that the AirPods Pro 3 were canceling out fine when I first got them. And even without any active noise, the background noise you hear at times would just sound slightly echoey or hollow as well. Now, at first I thought, well, maybe this is just a fit issue, or maybe the AirPods Pro just got dirty. I did the ear tip test. I actually tried a size up and size down, and the issues still occurred. So, it's clearly not a fit issue. I got the AirPods cleaning kit from Apple and cleaned them following Apple's instructions. That didn't fix it either. Okay, so maybe it's just a software glitch. So, I unpaired them from my iPhone and other Apple devices and I reset them by doubletapping the front of the case continuously, which is how you do it now since there's no Bluetooth pairing button on the back. And that didn't resolve it either. So, at that point, I went to Reddit and sure enough, other users were complaining of similar issues. But given how many AirPods Pro Apple sells, and even though there were numerous posts about this, I figured perhaps all of these people, myself included, we just got faulty pairs of AirPods. So, a few weeks ago, like any sane person would do, I bought another pair of AirPods Pro 3. I took them out of their case, put them in, and everything sounded great. The noise cancellation was perfect for about 45 minutes and then there it was, that hollow background noise again. So, at this point, it's very unlikely that I just got two faulty pairs of AirPods that were manufactured months apart from each other. So, I went back to Reddit and realized I had missed a key detail that someone had pointed out. When this hollow noise appears, if you trigger transparency mode and then switch back to noise cancellation again, it apparently disappears. And sure enough, that was my experience as well, making it likely that this issue that I've been plagued with is actually a software issue. Or at least that's my best guess at this point. And if you've noticed a similar issue with your AirPods Pro 3 or just had the noise cancellation degrade but don't notice that hollow sound as much, do definitely let me know in the comments. And if you want to see more long-term reviews like this one, by the way, a thumbs up on this video does go a long way to supporting us, as well as, of course, making sure you're subscribed. All right, so I can just end the video now and say I don't recommend these, right? It's actually a little bit more complicated than that. Uh, one, this is an issue that I do not think is affecting everybody who bought uh these AirPods. Otherwise, you'd be seeing even way more threads and comments about this issue online. So, either this is an issue that is only affecting certain people's uh AirPods, maybe it's like the internal mics and different people's ear shapes are interacting with them differently. So, that's why some people experience it while others don't. Or this is an issue that is impacting everybody's AirPods, but only certain people notice it or can hear it. With audio, audio is kind of weird. People perceive audio differently. I could also see that argument being plausible as well. But the real reason I just can't say I 100% don't recommend these is even with the issue I've had with them. I find myself still using them quite often because of the highlights like noise cancellation when it's actually working. These buds are quite a bit better than the AirPods Pro 2, blocking out more background noise, especially for higher frequencies that I could actually use them while cooking, which I could never do with the Pro 2 because they didn't block out enough of those higher frequency noises. Overall, I think the AirPods Pro 3 noise cancellation is actually right in line with my new Sony WF1000 XM6s, which I'm currently reviewing. Now, given that the XM 6s don't have that degradation issue that I've had with the AirPods Pro 3, plus they come with foam tips that are all foam tips and just still give you better passive noise isolation overall. I think they still remain the better option for noise cancellation. Now, the AirPods Pro 3 did introduce some foam into their tips, which is part of why their noise cancellation is better than the previous generation, but they still have that classic silicon outer layer. I've noticed with the AirPods Pro 3 specifically, after long listening sessions, I'll notice my ear canal will have just like a tiny layer of condensation. Like not like beaded water or anything like that, but you can just tell that there's a little bit of condensation in there. That's something I did not notice with my previous generation of AirPods as much and something I also don't get with the Sony's. That could be something that's contributing to this hollow noise since the condensation appearance often correlates with that hollow noise. Though, of course, correlation does not necessarily equal causation. Sound quality is another highlight. Apple gave the AirPods Pro 3 a new multiport acoustic architecture that helps precisely control the air flow, carrying sound into your ear, plus next generation adaptive EQ. Together, those give the AirPods Pro 3 a slightly wider sound stage compared to the Pro 2. Now, you'll also notice a bit more detail in your music. And at the low end, I think the bass is a bit better as well, though not overly heavy or muddy. Mic quality is also excellent, as is their transparency mode. Something Apple just seems to be able to do better than anybody else in this category. All right, so here's a mic test of the AirPods Pro 3. There's not a ton of like uh background noise or anything. car is about to come by me here so you can see how well their mics cancel out. Uh this is what their mics sound like. And if you've only used AirPods, it is really easy to not appreciate just how good this transparency mode is compared to a lot of the competition. It doesn't even sound like you're wearing earbuds. The only giveaway is just feeling the pressure in my ear. Otherwise, the audio that I'm hearing sounds exactly like my voice sounds when I'm not wearing earbuds. It's crazy good. And fit is another highlight. Now, in the past, I found the AirPods Pro to not be the most comfortable earbuds, but I think they've really improved things here. Notice that they changed the location of where the ear tips stick out of the bud, so less of the bud actually has to go into your ear canal. I think that's why I've been able to wear them for hours without any discomfort. And that's certainly something I could not do with the original AirPods Pro or even the AirPods Pro 2, which could make my ears sore after a long listening session. And another improvement Apple made with the AirPods Pro 3 is battery life. They have noticeably better battery life at 8 hours with&c on compared to the 6 hours I got with the AirPods Pro 2. and I haven't had any issues with the battery life so far. Another feature I've loved about these buds is the find my integration and that precision finding feature where you can use your phone and the U1 chip to find the AirPods uh using ultra wideband. That's the technology that makes this work. It is so useful and it works so reliably and is so good. If you've only ever known AirPods, it's so easy to not appreciate how good this feature is compared to other buds. And the fact that this case has a speaker in it just makes them so much easier to actually be able to find. So, those are the major things I've really liked about them. Though, there are a few more features I haven't used all that much that I still want to mention. First is heart rate monitoring. It's great for those who don't want to wear a watch to track their heart rate during a workout, but because I do wear an Apple Watch, it's not something I've really utilized for workouts. The live translation feature got a lot of headlines, but it's still in beta and is limited in the languages that it supports. Plus, that's another feature that I haven't utilized or even really been able to test in my daily life. But I'll leave a few videos of that specific feature and people's experience with it in the learn more section of our blog post. There you'll also find all of the products linked in this video, plus links to additional retailers and links to our store page where you can see all of the products that we recommend. The only other downside I still have with AirPods is this case. Because it is so glossy, sometimes it can be a little slippery, slip out of your hand or pocket, and then it scratches so easily. It'll scratch within like the first day you have this thing in your pocket. And it scratches so much it almost forms this kind of like used patina on it, which honestly really doesn't bother me all that much, but if you don't like seeing a lot of scratches on your tech products, could definitely bother you. So, given the fairly major issues I've had with the AirPods Pro 3, if I had known about that going in, would I have still bought them? And would I recommend that you get a pair now? Well, if you're specifically looking for AirPods, I still would recommend them. primarily because even though this annoying bug that I run into from time to time, usually after wearing them for an extended period, maybe 45 minutes to an hour or so on average, I do still find myself wearing them constantly. And that's because even with this issue, I like their fit, sound quality, how easy they are to connect to my Apple devices, the great spatial audio you get when paired to an Apple TV or other Apple device. So, for the times that I want to use AirPods, out of all the AirPods I could potentially reach for, the AirPods Pro 3 are still the ones I pick up every time. And the reason for that is they just sound better. They've got better noise cancellation, a better fit, and longer battery life. Now, yes, of course, I wish they were more perfect than they actually are, but their downsides haven't led me to not actually want to use them, which is why, yes, if you're looking for AirPods that aren't headphones, they still ultimately do get my recommendation. Now, if you're between these and the AirPods 4 with A&C, I have done a video specifically comparing those two that I'll leave here if you want to dive deeper into that topic. Now, if you're looking for earbuds in general, I'd still look at the Sony WF1000 XM6s, which have really great sound and excellent noise cancellation, though for me a slightly worse fit. So, those are all of my thoughts on the AirPods Pro 3 after 6 months. Let me know what you think of them in the comments, and make sure you check out our website, sixmonser.net, for all of the latest on the tech that we cover. And for deals on Apple products or even specifically AirPods, check out our shop page. Just search Apple deals or AirPods deals to see if any of those products that we recommend have currently gone on sale. For 6 months later, I'm Josh Tedar. An

[음악] 그래서 전반적으로 평가를 하자면 프로투드에 비 3는 확실히 디테일과 분리감을 강조하는 느낌이었고요. 프로토는 둥글둥글하게 듣기 편한 사운드였습니다. 다음은 통화 품질입니다. 에어팟이 새로운 모델이 나올 때마다 통화 품질로는 그 당시 최고의 증찬을 받았지만 프로트는 나온지 3년이 되었기 때문에 지금 기준으로 봤을 때 그렇게 좋은 동화 품질은 아니긴 해요. 그래서 요것도 세 개를 한번 비교해 봤습니다. 애플 파이너 컷 카메라 2.0 공개. 아이폰 17프로 및 아이폰 17프로맥스의 프로레즈 로우 및 젤락 지원 기능과 신규 아이폰 라인업의 센터 스테이지 전면 카메라를 활용해 크게 도약한다. 전체적으로 새 제품 통합 품질은 괜찮았거든요. 근데 버저 3프로랑 에어팟 프로 2가 조금 먹에 비해 에어팟 프로 3는 가장 선명하게 들렸습니다. 근데 어떻게 보면 전자 느낌이 조금 심하게 느껴지기도 했고요. 그리고 이번에 새롭게 추가된게 아까 말했던 것처럼 이제 신박수 측정 기능이 이번에 새롭게 들어갔잖아요. 그래서 요게 장점이 뭐냐면 이제는 애플워치 없이도 운동 트래킹이 가능합니다. 그래서 요렇게 이제 착용을 하고 나서 우리가 많이 쓰는 피트니스 앱으로 들어갔을 때 iOS 26으로 업데이트가 안 되어서 지금 안 뜨거든요. iOS 26을 업데이트 하면 여기에 새롭게 추가된 기능이 됩니다. 그럼 여기서 애플러워치에서 운동 트래킹 간 것처럼 또 선택을 해서 운동을 할 수 있어요. 예전에 제가 찾아보기론 귀에서 측정하는게 손목보다 더 정확하다고들은 거 같거든요. 그대도 애플에서 물어보기로는 이제 에어팟만 착용하고도 운동 트래킹 신박수나 칼로리 데이터를 받을 수도 있지만 애플로워치와 동시에 착용하게 된다면 둘 중에 더 정확한 데이터를 쓴다고 해요. 에프러워치 시리즈 10 갤럭시 워치 8 에어팟 프로를 각각 다른 기기에 연결하고 10분 정도 뛰었을 때 고차범위가 플러스 마이너스 5 정도로 꽤나 정확했습니다. 나머지는 배터리 타임도 좀 증가했습니다. 유닛만 단독으로 봤을 때 6여시간 사용 가능한게 8시간으로 더 늘어났고요. 심지어 여기서 신박수 측정 기능을 켜더라도 기정보다 30분 더 오래 가는 배터리 타임입니다. 근데 또 케이스 사용 시간은 더 줄어들었어요. 30시간에서 24시간으로 줄어들었거든요. 그래서 요걸 보면 유닛은 약간 두꺼워지고 무게가 늘면서 배터리가 조금 더 늘어난 것 같고 케이스는 커졌는데 무게가 줄면서 배터리 용량을 낮춘 거 같기도 합니다. 근데 이제 실용에서 이제 비교를 하자면 케이스의 배터리가 더 느는게 좋냐, 유닛의 배터리가 더 느는게 좋냐, 요거를 비교하면 유닛이긴 하지만 둘 다 느는게 더 좋긴 해요. 또 한편으로 어떻게 보면 기존에서 6시간 사실 6시간 네대 착용하기는 좀 어렵거든요. 그 사이에 한번 케이스에 넣으면 또 빨리 충전되다 보니까 뭐 요거는 그게 중요하진 않다. 그리고 새롭게 들어간 실시간 번역 기능. 제가 현장에서 봤을 때 보니까 가능한 언어에 한국어가 없었습니다. 야 이거 한국어 추가되려면 또 1년 기다려야 되나 했는데 데모 브리핑에 가니까 한국어로 제가 체험을 해 볼 수 있었어요.요 한국어를 켜잖아요. 그럼 기본적으로 일단 번역을 했을 때 노이즈 캔슬링으로 상대방 목소리를 줄이고요. 번역되는 언어가 내 귀로 들리는 그런 방식이었어요. >> 실제로 한국어 번역도 꽤 잘 됐어요. 그러니까 이게 소통하기 무리 없을 정도. 심지어 실시간 번역을 쓰잖아요. 그럼 아이폰에서 번역에 비면서 또 요거를 문자로 볼 수도 있었고요. 요즘 우리 AI가 많이 발전하면서 LLM이라고 해서 뭐 GPT나 재미나이 보면 내가 처음에 물었던 거 외에도 큰틀을 보면서 앞뒤에 문맥을 파악하면서 대답해 주는 것에 비해 얘는 그냥 우리가 일반 번역을 하는 것처럼 앞뒤에 문맥을 파악하지 못하다 보니까 그 점이 좀 단점이었어요. 그리고 기본적인 H2칩은 동일하지만 광대역 칩이 U1에서 U2로 업그레이드 되었습니다. 요것도 재밌는게 뭐냐면 애플에서 첫 번째 울트라 와이드 밴드 초광대역 칩을 유원이라고 정의를 했어요. 근데 아이폰 15대 요거를 계산하면서 2세대 초광제역 칩이라고 언급을 했거든요. 근데 이번 에어팟 프로 3에서는 또 유트라고 하더라고요. 그러니까 이게 약간 좀 족보가 꼬인 거지. 그래서 스펙도 보잖아요. 그럼 2세대 초광대역치비 유원 대비 최대 세배 인식 거래가 늘어났고 그래서 요것도 2세대 초광대역 칩이 그냥 이번에 유원칩으로 이름이 다시 바뀐게 아닐까 같은 칩이 아닐까 해서 찾아봤거든요. 근데 좀 다른 거 같더라고요. 왜냐면 유원칩을 탑재한 프로 2 대비 1.5배 인식 범위가 더 넓고 향상된 인식은 또 새로운 아이폰 에어 17시리즈에서만 지원한다는 것을 보면 이게 좀이 이상해요. 아무튼 오늘은 많은 분들께서 기다리신 에어팟 프로에 대해서 살펴봤습니다. 전반적인 평가로 하자면 노이즈 캔슬링은 이미 프로투도 좋았기 때문에 미친듯한 체감은 아니었고 오히려 음질적인 부분에서의 체감이 저는 더 크게 다가왔습니다. 그리고 운동하시는 분들은 신박수 측정 기능이 들어갔기 때문에 요즘도 좀 좋을 것 같고요. 특히 제가 아침에 운동 가다 보면 에프러워치를 안 들고 가서 아 신박수 측정해야 되는데 칼로리 측정해야 되는데 이렇게 하면서 다시 집에 갔다가 나오는 케이스도 많았거든요. 근데 에어팟은 보통 운동할 때 음악을 대부분 듣다 보니까 고점도 좀 편해지지 않을까? 다만 한 가지 걱정이라면 착용감의 호보로가 좀 많이 심할 거라는 거. 저는 지금 요렇게 간단하게 체험해 봐도 프로 3를 끼울 때마다 큐가 아파. 아무튼 오늘 여기까지요. 아까 말한 것처럼 착용감에서 크게 불편함이었다면 장반적으로 모든 부분에서 업그레이드이다.

judgement.community_consensus: 에어팟 프로3는 음질과 노캔 성능이 크게 향상되었으며, 심박 센서 추가 등 혁신적인 기능이 포함되어 있어 출퇴근 사용자와 ANC를 중시하는 사용자에게 적합하다. 다만 착용감에 대한 호불호가 분명히 나뉘며, 순수 음질을 중시하는 사용자에게는 아쉬움이 있을 수 있다.
judgement.best_use_case: 도심 출퇴근 시 노이즈 캔슬링과 심박 센서를 활용한 운동 트래킹이 필요한 사용자
reviewer_claims: 에어팟 프로3는 음질과 노캔 성능이 크게 향상되었다
심박 센서 추가로 운동 트래킹 기능이 강화되었다
착용감에 따라 호불호가 갈릴 수 있다
배터리 수명이 유닛은 증가했으나 케이스는 감소했다
실시간 번역 기능은 일부 한계가 있다
community_reactions: 노이즈 캔슬링 성능에 대한 긍정적 평가
심박 센 Sensor 기능에 대한 기대감

As a clear braces user, I can tell Linus is going through the "try not to eat anything with food dye" phase.
Whoever on the editing team used Gojira's "The Way of All Flesh" as the example for favorite metal album...you're good people, that album is nothing but bangers
Yellow has to be the most ill advised colour choice for braces.

1:33 Just got my first Airpods pro 3. I was biased by Linus’s review. In particular about being difficult to remove the tips. And it was difficult until… I decided to watch Apple’s video on how to remove them. By following the instructions, it was quite easy! Who would have thought!
Librepods requiring root is actually a bug in Android. And “should” be fixed soon. Assuming Google gives a hoot — because not all Android devices require it to be root.

10:05 this is subject to be changed. The reason it requires root acces is because of a bug inside android which has been reported more than a year ago. 

It has gotten a tiny bit of atention from the Google team when the bug was posted but they haven't even responded once for months now.

If this ever gets fixed, root access is not needed anymore.
That Tim Cook impression was brilliant

My wife got me a pair of these because I am having hearing issues with my left ear. This was a huge upgrade from my non pro AirPods… I am using them almost as hearing aids for noisy environments, with adaptive mode, which evens out loud sounds and helps clean up conversations. 

ANC is amazing!
1:42 I didn't know I needed a Tim Cook impression from Riley today, but I'm thankful I've seen one

The AirPods Pro 3 are my first pair of AirPods ever because of the fit, these ones actually stay in my ear unlike the previous models and the ANC is truly top notch not even the AirPods Max come close. I’ve listened to both back to back and it’s not even a competition. For sound quality they are very head-to-head but for ANC transparency AirPods Pro 3 is where it’s at not to mention call quality which the AirPods Pro 3 win hands down
10:46 Did not expect to see my home countries GDP on a LTT video
I love when people say negative stuff about newer things. Makes me feel better for not upgrading from my AP Pro 2s.
As someone who makes a lot of ear wax. I immediately punctured the film on the ear tip trying to clean it with a cotton swab. Almost never had that happened on the AirPod pros 2.

5:35 There is a adaptive mode where you can choose how much noise bleeds through the ANC, it's like a between ANC and transparency. It's called "Adaptive Mode" in the anc menu on apple in the control center with the volume control.

How is the wind rejection? Found it lacking in the pro 2 and 4+anc. Just picks up any little wind gust and makes it impossible to hear my music. No one seems to ever talk about it on any headphone review, not just for airpods. But differences in brands are large on that part.
The best thing imo is transparency mode. I've never had headphones that were able to completely block wind noice while still allowing me to hear my surroundings. It's absolutely amazing.
1:07 My AirPods Pro 2s were a gamechanger as a fulltime woodworker and welder. Theyre even rocking after dropping them into metal dust more times than id like to admit...

My main gripe with the pro 3 compared to the pro 2 and 1 is that literally no ear tip fits my ear. I had no issues with the base tip in either of the other pros I had. Pro 3s just fall slowly slide out of my ears over the course of 30 minutes or so of low movement. Can't work out with them even on an elliptical without them immediately sliding out. Was so excited for these too from the expectations I had from the first 2 pros.

I appreciated your honesty in the first video. We SHOULD be pissed off when Apple Overlords mess up their product and don’t allow custom EQ when they very easily could. Noise cancellation is the biggest factor for me, as I already have some hearing damage, so I will probably upgrade when I get a new phone. But I’m going to make doubly sure now that I can tolerate the sounds signature, knowing if they sound a bit funny, Linus warned me first and I  have every right to return them.

I have a genuine theory that a lot of people that are in charge of adjusting the tuning of in-ear audio stuff are actively going deaf with so much treble so thats why they keep cranking it even more.

For the trend towards a heavy bass focus, is largely because it is popular with focus group tests. The main reasoning is for people who want to use them outdoors and in public transportation as well as in vehicles with lots of road noise. Since most of that noise content is in the lower frequency range, It often changes your perception of bass, thus a V curve helps maintain the sense of bass in those environments.
One issue is apple doesn't do as good of a job with a compensated V curve compared to companies like Bose where the EQ changes with volume level as well as with the environment, it is also why their EQ is so limited (3 bands). I am not sure why doesn't implement the same thing (not sure of Bose has any patent on it or not).

ranked.title: AirPods Pro 3 Review in 60 Seconds!
ranked.description:
This is our 60 seconds reviews of AirPords Pro 3 with pros and cons, and we are doing this review after using these earbuds for over 2 weeks now. Apple claims “world’s best ANC”, and when you put them on, you really feel it. Your annoying colleague’s voice completely shuts down and even when you are on the road, the traffic noise is barely audible and when you start playing music, you can be in your world. To achieve this, Apple has redesigned the buds. The eartips are now foam-infused, so you get a much better seal and they are now IP57 rated too. They also come with a custom PPG sensor to track your heart rate, which is pretty accurate, and you can even see the calories burnt, just by using the buds. There’s also a new Live Translation feature that works “fine”, but the languages are very limited. There’s no Indian language as of yet. When it comes to the sound quality? The bass is super good and punchy, mids are crisp and the treble is also well-extended. But in certain songs, the bass is overpowering the vocals, so it’s not for everyone. So, if I have to rank them, bass-wise, AirPods Pro 3 is on top, but in overall sound quality, Sony WF-1000XM5 are still the best. Other than that, I love the new improved spatial audio, as the head tracking works perfectly, and the Transparency mode also feels very natural and spot on. Lastly, the battery life on the buds have also increased to 10 hours now, and the case gets an upgraded UWB chip for faster finding. And yeah, there are no buttons this time, you just have to double tap on the front for pairing. Finally, if you are wondering about the mic quality, it's also very loud and crisp, be it inside or outside. Overall, Apple has really nailed the AirPods Pro 3 this time.

Subscribe to @beebomco for more such cool 60-Second tech reviews!
